NTEYkog6lI6lKEQJAAAAAA==.title: Senior Network Engineering Specialist
NTEYkog6lI6lKEQJAAAAAA==.description:
Job Description

What is the opportunity?

As a Senior Network Engineering Specialist, you will provide Subject Matter Expertise (SME) on F5 Load Balancers, DNS, CDN. You will advise appropriate design to application owners and stakeholders to onboard new applications on load balancer platforms.

Provide support with technical escalations, incidents, and project management. The experienced analyst may provide recommendations and contribute to the solutions in an area of expertise including leading work streams that are part of a medium initiative.

What will you do?
• Provide Subject Matter Expertise (SME) on F5 Load Balancers, DNS, CDN.
• Advise appropriate design to application owners and stakeholders to onboard new applications on load balancer platforms.
• Responsible for end-to-end change management leading to completion and resolution.
• Documentation and addressing Risk & Compliance tasks within the team.
• Lead day-to-day implementation and support.

What do you need to succeed?

Must have:
• University Degree in Network Engineering or a related field or Equivalent years of experience.
• 7+ years of experience in the following technologies: F5 Load Balancers, DNS, CDN.

Nice to have:
• ITIL v3: Foundations, Operational Support & Analysis
• Programming language experience (ex. Python)

What’s in it for you?

We thrive on the challenge to be our best, progressive thinking to keep growing, and working together to deliver trusted advice to help our clients thrive and communities prosper. We care about each other, reaching our potential, making a difference to our communities, and achieving success that is mutual.
• A comprehensive Total Rewards Program including bonuses and flexible benefits, competitive compensation, commissions, and stock where applicable.
• Leaders who support your development through coaching and managing opportunities.
• Ability to make a difference and lasting impact.
• Work in a dynamic, collaborative, progressive, and high-performing team.
• A world-class training program in financial services.
• Opportunities to do challenging work.

#LI-POST

#TECHPJ

Job Skills
Decision Making, Group Problem Solving, Incident Management, IP Technology, IT Troubleshooting, Network Engineering, Network Reliability Engineering, Wireless Communications Systems, Wireline Communications Technologies

Additional Job Details

Address:

RBC CENTRE, 155 WELLINGTON ST W:TORONTO

City:

Toronto

Country:

Canada

Work hours/week:

37.5

Employment Type:

Full time

Platform:

TECHNOLOGY AND OPERATIONS

Job Type:

Regular

Pay Type:

Salaried

Posted Date:

2026-05-12

Application Deadline:

2026-05-31

Note: Applications will be accepted until 11:59 PM on the day prior to the application deadline date above

Our Employment Opportunities

At RBC, we are guided by living shared values of Client First, Integrity, Collaboration, Respect and Excellence and winning together as One RBC. We believe an inclusive workplace that has diverse perspectives is core to our continued growth as one of the largest and most successful banks in the world. Maintaining a workplace where our employees feel supported to perform at their best, effectively collaborate, drive innovation, and grow professionally helps to bring our Purpose to life and create value for our clients and communities. RBC strives to deliver this through policies and programs intended to foster a workplace based on respect, belonging and opportunity for all.

Join our Talent Community

Stay in-the-know about great career opportunities at RBC. Sign up and get customized info on our latest jobs, career tips and Recruitment events that matter to you.

Expand your limits and create a new future together at RBC. Find out how we use our passion and drive to enhance the well-being of our clients and communities at jobs.rbc.com.

RBC is presently inviting candidates to apply for this existing vacancy. Applying to this posting allows you to express your interest in this current career
NTEYkog6lI6lKEQJAAAAAA==.reason: Requires 7+ years F5/DNS/CDN specialization which candidate lacks; their BGP/MPLS/CGNAT experience doesn't transfer to this application delivery focus.

_yjhyhvXjWn1zbmLAAAAAA==.title: Network Tools - Automation Engineer
_yjhyhvXjWn1zbmLAAAAAA==.description:
We Are: Accenture’s Infrastructure Engineering (IE) North America are dedicated to strategically solving our client’s cloud services, data center, digital workplace and operating model IT transformation challenges. We create predictability for technology intensive environments by applying proven engineering concepts and practices for long-term solutions. Our team consists of visionary infrastructure transformers, with a multidisciplinary team of technologists, financial analysts, project and program managers. We foster critical thinking and innovation that drive career development. Our reward is in a culture of collaborative experts who drive our industry forward. If you are motivated by turning challenging ideas into reality, creating uncharted value for clients and bringing your best self to your work – we want to connect with you.

Work You’ll Do:

We are looking for a Network Tools – Automation Engineer with a strong core networking background to design, implement, integrate, and stabilize network and security platforms across client environments. This role requires a strong foundation in enterprise and data center networking, proficiency operating network tools, and the ability to design tool-to-tool integrations using APIs, webhooks, and custom scripts. The engineer will drive operational maturity by delivering repeatable integrations, improving data quality across systems, and enabling observability and automation outcomes aligned to production support needs.

Responsibilities:

• Design and implement integrations between network/security tools and enterprise platforms using REST APIs, webhooks, and custom scripts, ensuring reliable data flow and operational stability

• Implement, operate, and stabilize network observability and security tooling (monitoring/visibility/SIEM-aligned integrations), including onboarding, tuning, and integration validation in production environments

• Develop and maintain Cisco ACI data center fabric via automation, APIC controller and Cisco Nexus Dashboard

• Develop and maintain documentation, SOPs, runbooks, and integration specs to enable repeatable deployments, supportability, and knowledge transfer

• Automate operational tasks with Python and scripting (JSON/PowerShell/API workflows), supporting data extraction, transformation, reporting, and controlled change execution across integrated platforms

• Conduct technical assessments of client current-state infrastructure

• Plan, implement, test and deploy automation across network and security solutions stacks:

• SD-WAN, SD-Access, SD-DC

• Firewalls, IDS/IPS

• Tool Observability

• Automation Pipelines

• Programmatic Networks

• Improve network security posture through micro and macro segmentation, policy optimization, and monitoring

• Improve resiliency through optimized failover and disaster recovery

• Validate AI-assisted analysis with logic, experience, and domain knowledge

Core Technical Experience:

• Enterprise Networking: LAN/WAN/DC/WLAN concepts; routing/switching fundamentals

• Software‑Defined Networking: SD‑WAN platforms, SD-Access,SD-DC, ISE, Catalyst Center (DNAC), Cisco ACI

• Programmatic Networking: Python, JSON, PowerShell; API calls and automation workflows

• Tool Integration & Automation: REST APIs, webhooks, authentication patterns, data mapping, error handling

• Infrastructure as Code: Ansible, Terraform, CI/CD

• Network Management tools: SolarWinds, Dynatrace, Datadog, Thousand Eyes

Basic Qualifications:

• 3+ years of experience in network engineering, network tooling, automation, or security operations with a strong enterprise network foundation

• 2+ years of scripting capability with Python plus supporting languages/tools (JSON, PowerShell); able to create maintainable automation and integration utilities

• Demonstrated ability to build and support integrations (API/webhook/event-driven or scheduled sync patterns) across platforms and environments

• Proven capability to operate with Data normal
_yjhyhvXjWn1zbmLAAAAAA==.reason: Strong direct match - network engineer with relevant vendor experience (Cisco/Juniper) seeking network ops/engineering role requiring automation and enterprise networking skills.

dh22RO_9kXs29GL7AAAAAA==.title: Wireless Network Engineer
dh22RO_9kXs29GL7AAAAAA==.description:
• Should have better understanding of OSI Layer model and Packet Flow,

• Wireless Networks -Meraki and Cisco WLC and Access Points

• Minimum of 7 years of experience in deploying, managing, and tuning Wi-Fi networks in a corporate and manufacturing environment

• Strong proficiency in Cisco wireless technologies, including experience with Cisco 9800 WLC and 9120/9130 Access Points

• Solid understanding of Meraki and Aruba wireless technologies, with hands-on experience in managing and troubleshooting these platforms

• Proven ability to plan and execute large-scale Wi-Fi deployments across multiple locations

• Roles and responsibilities:

• Essential Duties and Responsibilities:

The primary functions of the Senior Network Engineer, include (but are not limited to):

o Provide day-to-day operation support, monitoring, maintenance, updates, triage, and troubleshooting for Enterprise Networking environment

o Engage in solutions research and deployment, systems standards, internet services, capacity planning, Disaster Recovery, platform upgrades\modernization, and process improvement.

o Contribute to design of network related solutions and partner with Information Security and Compliance in review of all solutions against corporate security and compliance standards and requirements

o Design, implement, maintain, and support Network Operational monitoring\alerting platform(s) including Business driven dashboards, metrics, and KPI reporting to facilitate clear communication of network related performance across the enterprise

o IP Address space design

o External facing infrastructure, fault-tolerance, redundancy, DR, etc.

• Work with internal departments and clients to determine requirements and then execute\delegate required plans\steps complete implementation.

o lead efforts with cross-functional teams on enterprise network impacting initiatives and implementations

o Oversee Network-related projects and initiatives planning and implementation

o Work with required areas and support staff to facilitate appropriate logistical planning, communication, and execution of network related initiatives

o Collaborate across business areas to assure compilation of necessary business requirements from stakeholders

o Review and assess impact of proposed changes to existing environment

• Participate in implementations and support of all open initiatives and implemented network related technology solutions

• Provide Mentorship, Training, Vision

o Provide technical leadership for network related technologies and solutions

o Work with Company leadership to develop medium- and long-term strategies as the company’s goals and needs evolve

o collaborate across the organization to perform solution impact assessment and set strategy for efforts with major business impact

o Mainta
dh22RO_9kXs29GL7AAAAAA==.reason: Requires 7+ years wireless-specific experience with Meraki/Cisco WLC; candidate has 3 years in wireline routing/switching with no wireless background.

1Taq1GFgqD_YG4kZAAAAAA==.title: Network Engineer Autonomous Systems
1Taq1GFgqD_YG4kZAAAAAA==.description:
Position: Network Engineer for Autonomous Systems
Mercor is looking for a Network Engineer to work remotely on data processes for autonomous systems.

Your role will involve enhancing AI model performance through effective data analysis and labeling.

As a contract Network Engineer with Mercor, you will leverage your experience with enterprise networks to handle real-world data evaluations. This position requires you to review logs, identify anomalies, and establish data pipelines while ensuring high data quality through effective collaboration with client teams. You'll operate independently to meet deadlines in a remote setting.

Key Responsibilities:

• Review network telemetry, logs, and event data

• Classify and label significant network behaviors

• Help structure data pipelines for large-scale use

• Ensure data quality through client team interaction

• Work asynchronously to accomplish project goals

Requirements:

• Proven experience in enterprise network engineering

• Comfort with interpreting logs and metrics

• Curiosity about the relationship between data and machine learning

• Availability of 30-40 hours/week remotely

• Strong analytical skills for data assessments

Contribute your network engineering skills to propel AI advancements at Mercor.
#J-18808-Ljbffr
1Taq1GFgqD_YG4kZAAAAAA==.reason: Strong network engineering background with ML thesis aligns well, but role is contract-only AI data labeling rather than core network operations.

W8eJth7772qpHMO1AAAAAA==.title: AWS & Network DevOps Engineer — SRE & Security
W8eJth7772qpHMO1AAAAAA==.description:
A leading pension plan organization in Toronto is looking for an experienced DevOps Engineer to support cloud infrastructure and networking solutions. You will collaborate with various teams to ensure alignment with organizational objectives and lead initiatives requiring technical expertise. The ideal candidate has over 5 years in IT Infrastructure, strong troubleshooting skills, and familiarity with AWS and networking technologies.

This role offers a competitive annual salary range of $71,000 - $109,000 CAD.
W8eJth7772qpHMO1AAAAAA==.reason: Strong technical match (networking, AWS knowledge likely) but candidate has only 3 years experience versus required 5+ years.

T92JIBVNdaEPBBt3AAAAAA==.title: Systems Engineer Cloud and Networking
T92JIBVNdaEPBBt3AAAAAA==.description:
Position: Systems Engineer for Cloud and Networking
Elevate your career as a Systems Engineer at Solidifi, focusing on Azure, Microsoft 365, and networking technologies. Drive systems optimization and support diverse IT infrastructure needs.

Solidifi, a leader in the residential lending industry, requires a Systems Engineer to manage its enterprise IT systems. This role emphasizes Microsoft 365 administration, Azure deployments, and effective management of SQL databases. Your expertise will be essential in maintaining network configurations and supporting cloud migration initiatives.

Key Responsibilities:

• Support and administer Microsoft 365 and its applications

• Deploy Azure resources and manage hybrid environments

• Configure networking technologies including switches and routers

• Manage SQL databases related to enterprise applications

• Oversee VMware and Azure virtual environments

Requirements:

• Minimum 7 years of systems engineering experience

• Bachelor’s degree or equivalent

• Proficient in cloud technologies like Azure and GCP

• Experienced in SQL database technologies

• Relevant certifications in Microsoft and networking preferred

Become a key player in Solidifi’s IT team, contributing to our innovative network management solutions.
#J-18808-Ljbffr
T92JIBVNdaEPBBt3AAAAAA==.reason: Candidate has strong networking skills but lacks the 7 years experience, Azure/M365 focus, SQL/VMware expertise, and systems engineering background this role requires.

un6PupodNa7-GA5BAAAAAA==.title: Network Engineer (Network Operations Engineer)
un6PupodNa7-GA5BAAAAAA==.description:
About the job Network Operations Engineer Network Operations Engineer (Hybrid)

Our client, a leading IT solutions provider of innovative IT solutions, specializes in delivering tailored services to businesses across a broad spectrum of industries. With a strong foothold in the Greater Toronto Area (GTA) and beyond, they offer expertise in assessing, designing, implementing, and managing comprehensive IT solutions on the following areas:

Data Center Optimization and Virtualization

Data Security and Protection

Managed IT Services

Network Infrastructure and Cybersecurity

They are seeking a Network Operations Engineer to join their team based in the Greater Toronto Area (GTA). In this role, you will be part of the Network Operations Center (NOC) team, collaborating with Network Specialists, Support Services Technicians, and the Manager of Support Services. You will be responsible for identifying customer requirements and executing recommendations provided by our client.

Additionally, you will resolve technical support issues within your specialization, support internal infrastructure systems, and provide on-call escalation when necessary. Resolve technical support issues within your specialization within the NOC. Plan and implement internal NOC systems.

Implement project services as required.

Provide Tier 3 network support with limited supervision.

Develop and maintain network configuration and documentation according to industry best practices.

Experience with Fortinet Firewall and Switch Installation and Administration.

Strong organizational and planning skills.

Bachelors degree in a technology-related field or equivalent experience.

Job Types: Full-time, Permanent Hybrid work model (2/3 days a week in office)

Language Requirement: French proficiency not required.

Schedule: 8-hour shifts, Monday to Friday #
un6PupodNa7-GA5BAAAAAA==.reason: Direct match for network engineering role with relevant NOC experience, proper education, and vendor expertise (Fortinet experience gap is minor).

uZOY-uidM_y9eBEFAAAAAA==.title: Mercor Remote Network Engineer Contract
uZOY-uidM_y9eBEFAAAAAA==.description: Step into a Remote Network Engineer position with Mercor, focusing on data annotation for Autonomous Systems. As a Network Engineer, you will analyze real-time data from enterprise networks, classify network behaviors, and aid in the creation of data pipelines for AI systems. The position revolves around ensuring data quality and improving network understanding for machine learning applications. You will be instrumental in feeding raw infrastructure data into AI models. Key Responsibilities: • Analyze logs and events from operational networks • Label and classify network behaviors and anomalies • Define structures for complex data pipelines • Interface with client teams for data relevance • Meet deadlines while working independently Requirements: • Experience in enterprise-level network engineering • Ability to interpret logs and event metrics • Interest in data's role in machine learning • Comfortable working asynchronously and independently • Commitment to 30-40 hours per week Utilize your network expertise and analytical abilities at Mercor to enhance AI technologies. jfRGbcHkH-U3tcxZAAAAAA==.title: Toronto Cybersecurity Network Engineer
jfRGbcHkH-U3tcxZAAAAAA==.description:
Become a Cybersecurity Network Engineer with OPSEU/SEFPO in Toronto, specializing in network infrastructure security. Utilize your expertise in cybersecurity tools to protect vital organizational data.
In this role, you will manage the organization’s cybersecurity landscape while deploying and maintaining network infrastructure components. Your responsibilities will encompass the management of firewalls, VPNs, and endpoint protection tools to ensure compliance and security across all platforms. Collaboration with cross-functional teams will be essential to enforce best practices and enhance security frameworks.
Key Responsibilities:
• Deploy and optimize network infrastructure with firewalls and VPNs
• Conduct log monitoring and threat analysis
• Design incident response strategies and audits
• Improve security documentation and incident response playbooks
• Engage in training sessions on cybersecurity awareness
Requirements:
• Bachelor’s degree in Cybersecurity or related discipline
• Minimum three years of hands-on experience in security
• Proficiency with tools such as FortiGate and Microsoft Defender
• Strong understanding of network security compliance standards
• Thorough knowledge of scripting for security automation
Utilize your technical skills to enhance cyber defense mechanisms at OPSEU/SEFPO in this critical role.
#J-18808-Ljbffr
jfRGbcHkH-U3tcxZAAAAAA==.reason: Has network engineering experience and relevant master's degree, but lacks the specific cybersecurity tools expertise (FortiGate, Defender) and hands-on security focus the role requires.

ziCI6ZyH9K-l8J5dAAAAAA==.title: Senior Linux Software Engineer (Networking & Systems) (10138)
ziCI6ZyH9K-l8J5dAAAAAA==.description:
Over 50,000 customers globally trust our end-to-end, cloud-driven networking solutions. They rely on our top-rated services and support to accelerate their digital transformation efforts and deliver unprecedented progress.

Become part of something big with Extreme! As a global networking leader, learn why there is no better time to join the Extreme team.

Position details

Title of position: Senior Linux Software Engineer (Networking & Systems)

Position type: Full time

Location: Thornhill, Toronto, Canada

Position reports to: Senior Manager of Development Engineering

Application deadline: Applications are being accepted on a rolling basis and this posting will remain open until filled.

Work authorization: Applicants must have legal authorization to work in the country where the role will be performed.

Key Responsibilities

• Design, develop, and maintain robust Linux-based software in alignment with engineering best practices.
• Analyse, debug, and optimize code to ensure performance, scalability, and reliability.
• Develop automation to streamline build, integration, and testing processes.
• Prepare and maintain technical documentation, including design specifications and APIs.
• Collaborate effectively with software, QA, and hardware teams to deliver high-quality solutions.

Required Qualifications

• 3–6 years of software engineering experience, primarily in Linux backend development.
• Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, or a related field.
• Strong proficiency in C/C++, Java, Python or system scripting
• Strong understanding of L2/L3 networking and protocols, experience with Linux system application and networking development
• Solid experience in multi-threading, inter-process communication (IPC), and memory management.
• Hands-on experience with Linux debugging and performance profiling tools.
• Familiarity with open-source development practices.
• Excellent communication skills and the ability to work independently and collaboratively.

Additional Preferred Qualifications

• Experience with RESTful API development and integration (yaml, Swagger, Postman)
• Messaging/streaming subsystems, like RabbitMQ and Apache Kafka
• SQL and NoSQL database design and administration, performance tuning, query optimization. Redis as in-memory caching and real-time messaging tool
• Knowledge of client/server authentication and certificate-based security mechanisms
• Familiarity with AI-driven development tools to support code implementation and review, test creation, and workflow automation

Equal Employment Opportunity

• Extreme Networks, Inc. is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or status as a protected veteran. We are committed to taking affirmative action to employ and advance in employment qualified protected veterans, including disabled veterans, recently separated veterans, active-duty wartime or campaign badge veterans, and Armed Forces service medal veterans.
• Extreme Networks also strives to prevent other, subtler forms of inappropriate behavior (for example, stereotyping) from ever gaining a foothold in our organization. Whether blatant or hidden, barriers to success have no place at Extreme Networks. We encourage people from underrepresented groups to apply.

Fair chance and background checks

• Extreme Networks will consider qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with the California Fair Chance Act, Los Angeles Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring Ordinance, Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers, Philadelphia Fair Criminal Record Screening Standards Ordinance, Illinois Human Rights Act, Cook County Human Rights Ordinance, Seattle Fair Chance Employment Ordinance, and the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance. An applicant's conviction history will not be considered u
ziCI6ZyH9K-l8J5dAAAAAA==.reason: Role requires 3-6 years of Linux software development experience in C/C++/Java/Python, but candidate's background is network operations/engineering, not software engineering.

W7VAWagsIe12VPvjAAAAAA==.title: NOC Engineer Senior
W7VAWagsIe12VPvjAAAAAA==.description:
Xanadu’s mission is to build quantum computers that are useful and available to people everywhere. At Xanadu, we are learners, innovators, researchers, collaborators and problem solvers. We are creating something that has never been built before.

What we are doing is extremely hard, the classic moon shot. Few people in their life will be able to be a part of something like this, where if we are successful, the technologies we develop will solve some of the world’s most challenging problems and literally change the world. And that is something to be excited about!

Your role and responsibilities

Xanadu is looking for a Cryogenic Engineer to lead designing, developing, and building Xanadu’s next generation cryostats as well as contribute to the build-out of our facility-level infrastructure. In this role, you won’t just design; you’ll own the entire hardware lifecycle, ensuring our cryogenic platforms are robust, scalable, and mission-ready. You will collaborate closely with internal teams, subcontractors, and external partners to design, configure, and improve deployable systems while providing operational support for existing platforms.

With your deep technical expertise and experience in low temperature engineering, hands‑on expertise with cryostats and related technologies, you will play a critical role in designing and enhancing the performance and scalability of our detector systems necessary for our quantum architecture. This role requires a hands‑on approach to low‑temperature engineering and a proven ability to manage integration efforts across multidisciplinary teams, as well as the ability to work effectively across disciplines in a fast‑paced environment. Develop new and innovative cryostats for our photon detector systems Independently operate and test cryogenic systems (e.g.

He-4 evaporator based, dilution refrigerators) including performance metrics, maintenance, and troubleshooting Work with cryogenic liquid and gas systems to achieve optimized cooling requirements Help deploy large-scale cryogenic facilities with high recovery and efficiency Work with our scientific and engineering teams to push the limits on state‑of‑the‑art detector technologies Basic qualifications and experience Master’s degree, in Mechanical Engineering, Applied Physics, or a closely related technical field with at least 5 years of cryogenic industry experience Experience leading and iterating on cryostat designs CAD and simulation experience for thermal/mechanical modeling (e.g. Solidworks) Extensive hands‑on experience operating cryostats, including cooldowns, troubleshooting, and maintenance Knowledge and experience with high vacuum systems for cryogenic use Liquid cryogen handling and distribution experience Knowledge of design and fabrication of liquid heat exchangers Experience operating a variety of fridges including pulse tube, liquid‑cryogen, sorption, and dilution refrigerators Thermal and mechanical engineering simulation experience, including knowledge of low-temperature material properties Self‑motivated, comfortable working both independently and collaboratively with people of all disciplines on complex projects in a fast‑paced environment Detail‑oriented with positive attitude towards work Excellent interpersonal and written communication skills Preferred qualifications and experience 7+ years cryogenic industry experience Experience with statistical process control and quality management system reporting methods Experience writing Python code Knowledge of Canadian and international regulations and standards for pressure and electrical systems This is for a new position. Your base salary will be determined based on your location, experience, and internal benchmarks.

The base salary range is 150,000 - 200,000 CAD. You will also be eligible for equity and benefits. Our values are important. They are fundamental and lay the foundation for culture at Xanadu. Learn more about our values here.

We are an equal opportunity empl
W7VAWagsIe12VPvjAAAAAA==.reason: Job requires cryogenic engineering expertise with mechanical/physics background; candidate has network engineering and computer science skills with zero relevant crossover.

K6NpAbvdtkk_WnU6AAAAAA==.title: Technical Sales Engineer - Fabrics & Rolls (Paper Machine Clothing)
K6NpAbvdtkk_WnU6AAAAAA==.description:
Every day, ANDRITZ continues to deliver successful innovative solutions to our customers globally. Why are we so successful? Because we are passionate and love what we do! We are at the forefront of future engineering technologies, with solutions that ensure the success of our clients in key industries that are shaping the future of the world we live in.

Location: Ontario, Canada (Greater Toronto Area preferred)

Territory: Ontario & Eastern Canada

Travel: Regional travel to customer mills required

About ANDRITZ

ANDRITZ Fabrics & Rolls is a global leader in paper machine clothing, roll technology, and performance optimization solutions for the pulp & paper industry. From the wet end to the dry end, our technologies help mills improve productivity, reliability, and operating efficiency.

We are seeking a Technical Sales Engineer to support our Fabrics & Rolls business in Ontario, partnering closely with paper mills to identify optimization opportunities, deliver value‑based solutions, and grow long‑term customer relationships.

What You’ll Do

As a Technical Sales Engineer, you will serve as a trusted technical advisor to paper mills, combining hands‑on paper machine expertise with consultative sales to improve customer performance and drive ANDRITZ growth.

Customer Engagement & Technical Sales

• Build and maintain strong relationships with paper mill customers across Ontario and Eastern Canada

• Identify opportunities for paper machine clothing, roll covers, bowed rolls, and related services

• Conduct paper machine audits, service calls, and optimization studies

• Support customers during shutdown inspections, troubleshooting, and performance improvement initiatives

Optimization & Performance Support

• Perform paper machine optimization and press section studies, including Rezolve™ press optimization

• Analyze machine performance using proprietary modeling and diagnostic tools

• Troubleshoot operating challenges and recommend practical, value‑driven solutions

• Proactively identify issues to reduce warranty exposure and improve long‑term machine performance

Reporting & Collaboration

• Prepare clear, professional on‑site reports, recommendations, and follow‑ups

• Maintain detailed customer and machine records, including PMC, rolls, installations, and product life data

• Communicate field activity, opportunities, and competitive insights to the Sales Manager

• Provide monthly summaries and forecasts highlighting opportunities, mill conditions, and territory priorities

What We’re Looking For

• Degree in Engineering, Business, or equivalent experience

• Hands‑on experience in paper mills (production, maintenance, reliability, process, or technical sales)

• Strong knowledge of paper machine clothing, press sections, rolls, or machine optimization

• Proven ability to build relationships and communicate effectively at all mill levels

• Entrepreneurial, self‑driven, and comfortable working independently in the field

• Strong computer skills (Word, Excel, databases / CRM)

• Ability to travel within the assigned Ontario / Eastern Canada territory

Preferred

• Experience with paper machine clothing, rolls, or press optimization

• Background in technical sales, applications engineering, or field service

• Familiarity with paper machine audits and shutdown support

Why Join ANDRITZ Fabrics & Rolls

• Work with a globally recognized leader in paper machine performance

• Combine technical expertise with customer impact

• Long‑term customer relationships and stable territory

• Competitive compensation and expense support

• Strong technical, sales, and service team collaboration

• Medical Dental,

• Vision

• Life Insurance

• Disability Insurance

• 401k Match

• Education Assistance

• Parental Leave
K6NpAbvdtkk_WnU6AAAAAA==.reason: Sales engineer role is in paper manufacturing industry requiring pulp/paper machine expertise, completely unrelated to candidate's networking and tech background.

51Yqm3T765ZZPIkWAAAAAA==.title: Senior Network Engineer at NTT DATA
51Yqm3T765ZZPIkWAAAAAA==.description:
Elevate your network engineering skills with NTT DATA. As a Senior Network Engineer, you will manage hybrid networks and optimize network security protocols in a flexible home office environment.

We seek a talented Senior Network Engineer with over ten years of experience, including five years in a senior capacity. Your expertise in network protocols and diagnostics is essential for optimizing both in-house and Azure cloud-based networks. This role demands advanced troubleshooting skills and the ability to work independently or in a team, tackling complex issues head-on.

Key Responsibilities:
• Design and deploy LAN, WAN, and WLAN networks
• Configure routers, switches, firewalls, and VPNs
• Perform ongoing network maintenance and upgrades
• Monitor system performance and ensure availability
• Document and maintain network configurations and diagrams

Requirements:
• 10+ years of network engineering experience
• 5+ years in a senior role
• Deep understanding of network protocols and OSI model
• Hands-on experience with monitoring tools
• Professional certifications in networking

Maximize network performance and reliability while mentoring IT team members at NTT DATA.
51Yqm3T765ZZPIkWAAAAAA==.reason: Candidate has relevant technical skills but only 3 years experience versus the required 10+ years with 5+ in senior capacity.

wFcIjaXhoyWXJivOAAAAAA==.title: Senior Engineer, Network Management, Global Deloitte Technology
wFcIjaXhoyWXJivOAAAAAA==.description:
Job Type: Permanent
Work Model: Remote
Reference code: 133403
Primary Location: Toronto, ON
All Available Locations: Toronto, ON; Kitchener, ON; Ottawa, ON; Vancouver, BC

Our Purpose

At Deloitte, our Purpose is to make an impact that matters. We exist to inspire and help our people, organizations, communities, and countries to thrive by building a better future. Our work underpins a prosperous society where people can find meaning and opportunity. It builds consumer and business confidence, empowers organizations to find imaginative ways of deploying capital, enables fair, trusted, and functioning social and economic institutions, and allows our friends, families, and communities to enjoy the quality of life that comes with a sustainable future. And as the largest 100% Canadian-owned and operated professional services firm in our country, we are proud to work alongside our clients to make a positive impact for all Canadians.

By living our Purpose, we will make an impact that matters.

• Have many careers in one Firm.

• Enjoy flexible, proactive, and practical benefits that foster a culture of well-being and connectedness.

• Learn from deep subject matter experts through mentoring and on the job coaching

Deloitte Global is the engine of the Deloitte network. Our professionals reach across disciplines and borders to develop and lead global initiatives. We deliver strategic programs and services that unite our organization.

What will your typical day look like?

• Provide operational support for Network Security Services across the Deloitte global infrastructure.

• 24x7 device monitoring, operation, and engineering.

• Participate in a rotational on-call coverage schedule

• First responder to monitored alerts, incidents, and issues.

• Familiarity with Palo Alto, Checkpoint, Infoblox, Bluecat, Cisco ISE technologies a plus

• Experience with SaaS applications, deployments, and migration to cloud services

• DNS, DHCP, and IPAM experience

• Excellent organizational, consulting, project management, and time management skills

• Ability to work both independently and in a team environment

• Successful experience in managing global enterprise customers

About the team

Deloitte Technology works at the forefront of technology development and processes to support and protect Deloitte around the world. In this truly global environment, we operate not in "what is" but rather "what can be" to help Deloitte deliver and connect with its clients, its communities, and one another in ways not previously conceived.

Enough about us, let’s talk about you

• Experience working with the InfoBlox and Bluecat: (DNS, DHCP, IPAM, NTP, Reporting, etc..)

• Experience working with Cisco ISE: (802.1x, TACACS, Radius)

• Knowledge of DNS, DHCP, Active Directory

• Knowledge of Palo Alto Prisma Access will be an added advantage.

• Knowledge of API’s is a plus

• Knowledge of various cloud platforms: (Azure, AWS, GCP)

• Knowledge of LAN/WAN and network protocols.

• Knowledge of monitoring tools and commands to ensure quick resolution to issues.

• Cisco certification are a plus (CCNA, CCNP)

• Palo certifications are a plus (PCCET, PCNSA/PCNSE)

Total Rewards

The salary range for this position is $69,000 - $114,000, and individuals may be eligible to participate in our bonus program. Deloitte is fair and competitive when it comes to the salaries of our people. We regularly benchmark across a variety of positions, industries, sectors, targets, and levels. Our approach is grounded on recognizing people's

uvVJdfmMNc0bL4r7AAAAAA==.title: Senior Network Engineer/Architect – Campus, Data Center & Security
uvVJdfmMNc0bL4r7AAAAAA==.description:
Who We Are

Applied Materials is a global leader in materials engineering solutions used to produce virtually every new chip and advanced display in the world. We design, build and service cutting-edge equipment that helps our customers manufacture display and semiconductor chips – the brains of devices we use every day. As the foundation of the global electronics industry, Applied enables the exciting technologies that literally connect our world – like AI and IoT. If you want to push the boundaries of materials science and engineering to create next generation technology, join us to deliver material innovation that changes the world.

What We Offer

Location:
Toronto,CAN

You’ll benefit from a supportive work culture that encourages you to learn, develop, and grow your career as you take on challenges and drive innovative solutions for our customers. We empower our team to push the boundaries of what is possible—while learning every day in a supportive leading global company. Visit our Careers website to learn more.

At Applied Materials, we care about the health and wellbeing of our employees. We’re committed to providing programs and support that encourage personal and professional growth and care for you at work, at home, or wherever you may go. Learn more about our benefits.

Summary

The Senior Network Engineer/Architect is a hands‑on technical leader responsible for designing, implementing, and operating secure, resilient, and scalable enterprise network infrastructure across campus, data center, cloud, manufacturing, OT, and Private 5G environments. This role blends architecture, deep engineering expertise, automation, and operational excellence while partnering closely with security, cloud, and application teams

Job Description

Applied's IT organization has a long reputation of being a great place to work. The IT team has been recognized as one of Computerworld's 100 Best Places to Work in IT. In addition, numerous Applied IT leaders have been honored as a CIO Magazine's Ones to Watch or Computerworld Premier 100 IT leaders.

We are seeking a Senior Network Engineer/Architect with deep expertise across WAN, LAN, Datacenter, SD‑WAN, Private 5G, and Sensor/IIoT connectivity to design, implement, and operate secure, resilient, and scalable enterprise network infrastructure.

This role is hands‑on and highly technical, with responsibility for architecture, engineering, lifecycle management, automation, and operational excellence across campus, data center, manufacturing, OT, R&D, and cloud‑connected environments.

The Senior Network Engineer/Architect partners closely with security, cloud, OT, and application teams to deliver reliable connectivity aligned with business priorities.

Core Competencies
• Interprets internal/external business issues and recommends best practices; solves complex problems with a broad perspective to identify innovative solutions. Works independently with guidance in only the most complex situations.
• Requires specialized depth of expertise in networking and security technologies.
• Proven design/engineering excellence across LAN, WAN, Data Center, SD‑WAN, NAC, WAN Optimization, Wireless, Firewalls, and advanced networking technologies (10+ years strongly preferred).
• Superior knowledge, skills, and experience with:
• Arista and Cisco Data Center switches (Leaf‑Spine; L2/L3 designs)
• Cisco switching/routing platforms and protocols
• Aruba products (campus switching and orchestration)
• SDWAN technologies (eg: Arista Velocloud)
• Palo Alto firewalls and advanced network security solutions
• Excellent knowledge/experience with networking technologies: Ethernet, ATM, MPLS, OSPF, IPsec, VLANs, HSRP, PPP, EIGRP, BGP, QoS, Multicast, ACLs, VPNs
• Experience using network management tools for performance tuning, troubleshooting, and capacity planning
• Working knowledge of security technologies and concepts
• Knowledge of voice infrastructure: IP Telephony, Telepresence
• Knowledge of scripting/a
uvVJdfmMNc0bL4r7AAAAAA==.reason: Strong network engineering background matches technical requirements, but lacks senior-level architecture experience and specific data center/security depth for this senior role.

2na4n7YcBoT_ulPkAAAAAA==.title: Senior Software Engineer Network at Okta
2na4n7YcBoT_ulPkAAAAAA==.description:
Contribute to global network architecture as a Senior Software Engineer at Okta. Join the Platform Network team to develop robust solutions for secure connectivity.

In this pivotal role, you will enhance the connectivity of Auth0 by designing multi-cloud networking infrastructure. Collaborating with a talented team, you will implement scalable solutions that accommodate millions of users while addressing operational and security challenges.

Key Responsibilities:
• Implement global-scale networking solutions for multi-cloud infrastructure
• Lead cross-team initiatives with code and design reviews
• Develop automation tools to improve network functionalities
• Research solutions for routing, scaling, and failover
• Participate in the team's on-call rotation

Requirements:
• 3+ years in software development, focusing on cloud-native services
• Strong knowledge of TCP/IP, DNS, HTTP, and TLS
• DevOps experience with cloud-agnostic technologies
• Familiarity with Terraform for infrastructure management
• Experience with Go-based service development

Bring your skills in network engineering and your passion for scalable designs to Okta's mission.
2na4n7YcBoT_ulPkAAAAAA==.reason: Candidate has strong networking knowledge but lacks the required software development and coding skills (Go, Terraform, DevOps) for this software engineering position.

9nMz03Pz9NAZTa1NAAAAAA==.title: Senior Staff Engineer - Network Operations
9nMz03Pz9NAZTa1NAAAAAA==.description:
Advance your engineering skills at Equinix as a Senior Staff Engineer in Network Operations, utilizing your expertise in Golang and cloud technologies to drive impactful solutions. Equinix is transforming its network operations and seeks a Senior Staff Engineer to build a unified network model. With a minimum of 7 years of experience, your role will involve designing and operating scalable network solutions that enhance reliability and customer service.

Collaborate with cross-functional teams to integrate your work into strategic initiatives such as Equinix Brain. Key Responsibilities:

• Participate in the design and development of network software
• Collaborate to identify insights from network data
• Lead discussions on software design trade-offs with stakeholders
• Promote best practices in software development
• Participate in on‑call rotations to support services Requirements:
• 7+ years in software services development with Golang
• Background in SaaS, PaaS, or cloud computing
• Experience with GCP Spanner and other data systems
• Knowledge of container technologies: Docker and Kubernetes
• Solid understanding of networking concepts and practices Utilize your skills to innovate and contribute to cutting-edge network solutions at Equinix.

eKMT83VKXvXAD0eWAAAAAA==.title: Principal Network Automation Engineer
eKMT83VKXvXAD0eWAAAAAA==.description:
Requisition ID: 245884

Join a purpose driven winning team, committed to results, in an inclusive and high-performing culture.

Scotiabank is seeking a highly skilled Principal Network Automation Engineer to join our Global Network Engineering (GNE) team. This role blends leadership of a small team of automation engineers with individual technical expertise within development of network automation and maintaining automation systems.

Network automation is primarily delivered through our in-house application, Argus, which is the source of truth for network inventory, handles compliance and provides reporting used for various purposes including hardware and software currency.

Is this role right for you?

• Financial institution experience, knowledge of bank processes

• Leadership experience, ability to set strategic goals and lead a team to deliver on them

• Ability to balance technical and business objectives and lead a team on both fronts

• Strong development skills, able to contribute

• Experience maintaining infrastructure

• Develop and maintain software tools for network automation and monitoring

• Design and implement network configurations and security policies

• Collaborate with cross-functional teams to develop solutions to optimize network activities including governance, compliance, audit, reporting, health checks, network implementations

• Contribute to pipelines and infrastructure-as-code deployments

• Document technical processes and maintain version control

Do you have the skills that will enable you to succeed in this role?

• Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, capable of:

• articulating complex technical concepts to non-technical audiences

• translating business objectives into clear technical objectives

• Strong interpersonal and relationships management skills

• Strong organizational skills

• Demonstrates proactivity and accountability

• Ability to analyze logs, metrics and performance data

• Knowledge of DevOps practices

• Skilled in coding languages: Python, Java, JavaScript, Anduril, Go (Golang)

• Hardware and software currency of network devices

• Vulnerability management

• Familiarity

• Networking technical knowledge:

• Core networking knowledge (routing & switching, load balancing, firewalls, DNS, etc)

• Familiarity with network vendors: Cisco, Fortinet, f5, Infoblox, Gigamon, NetBrain, etc

• Internet and Extranet DMZs

• SD-WAN

• Client and P2P VPN

• Cloud technologies

• Monitoring and network performance tools

• Degree or diploma in Computer Science or Computer Engineering, Telecommunications or Networking

• CCNA or CCNP certification is an asset

What's in it for you?
• Diversity, Equity, Inclusion & Allyship - We strive to create an inclusive culture where every employee is empowered to reach their fullest potential, respected for who they are, and are embraced through bias-free practices and inclusive values across Scotiabank. We embrace diversity and provide opportunities for all employee to learn, grow & participate through our various Employee Resource Groups (ERGs) that span across diverse gender identities, ethnicity, race, age, ability & veterans.
• Accessibility and Workplace Accommodations - We value the unique skills and experiences each individual brings to the Bank, and are committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive and accessible environment for everyone. Scotiabank continues to locate, remove and prevent barriers so that we can build a diverse and inclusive environment while meeting accessibility requirements.
• Upskilling through online courses, cross-functional development opportunities, and tuition assistance.
• Competitive Rewards program including bonus, flexible vacation, personal, sick days and benefits will start on day one.
• Community Engagement - no matter where you choose to work from; we offer opportunities for community engagement & belonging with our various programs such as hackathons, contests, cooking with friend
eKMT83VKXvXAD0eWAAAAAA==.reason: Strong network technical skills but lacks required leadership experience, financial institution background, and development/automation expertise for a principal-level role.

mz3i82oW2jQiIPHQAAAAAA==.title: Data-Focused Network Engineer Remote
mz3i82oW2jQiIPHQAAAAAA==.description:
Position: Data-Focused Network Engineer Remote Role
Become a pivotal part of Mercor as a Data-Focused Network Engineer. This contract position is fully remote and centers around improving network data for AI applications.

In this role, you'll be tasked with reviewing real-time data from networks and helping to ensure its quality for autonomous systems. Candidates should possess solid experience in managing enterprise network environments and be curious about the conversion of data into usable machine learning formats. Collaborating with a client team is essential for maximizing data relevance.

Key Responsibilities:

• Review and analyze deployed network logs and metrics

• Classify key issues and behaviors in network data

• Define structures for large data pipelines

• Collaborate with clients for quality assurance

• Independently manage tasks to meet set deadlines

Requirements:

• Experienced network engineer, familiar with enterprise systems

• Skilled in interpreting logs and time-series data

• Interest in machine learning applications of infrastructure data

• Ability to commit to 30-40 remote hours weekly

• Strong self-management and organizational skills

Your expertise will play a vital role in driving data quality and supporting AI model development at Mercor.
#J-18808-Ljbffr
mz3i82oW2jQiIPHQAAAAAA==.reason: Strong network engineering background with relevant ML thesis aligns well, but role's heavy data/AI focus is slightly outside current hands-on experience.

BYNZbho8bZVh1EPUAAAAAA==.title: Sr. Cloud Network Engineer, Global Deloitte Technology
BYNZbho8bZVh1EPUAAAAAA==.description:
Job Type: Permanent
Work Model: Remote
Reference code: 133404
Primary Location: Toronto, ON
All Available Locations: Toronto, ON; Ottawa, ON

Our Purpose

At Deloitte, our Purpose is to make an impact that matters. We exist to inspire and help our people, organizations, communities, and countries to thrive by building a better future. Our work underpins a prosperous society where people can find meaning and opportunity. It builds consumer and business confidence, empowers organizations to find imaginative ways of deploying capital, enables fair, trusted, and functioning social and economic institutions, and allows our friends, families, and communities to enjoy the quality of life that comes with a sustainable future. And as the largest 100% Canadian-owned and operated professional services firm in our country, we are proud to work alongside our clients to make a positive impact for all Canadians.

By living our Purpose, we will make an impact that matters.

• Have many careers in one Firm.

• Enjoy flexible, proactive, and practical benefits that foster a culture of well-being and connectedness.

• Learn from deep subject matter experts through mentoring and on the job coaching

-

Deloitte Global is the engine of the Deloitte network. Our professionals reach across disciplines and borders to develop and lead global initiatives. We deliver strategic programs and services that unite our organization.

What will your typical day look like?

• Architect and engineer the Global Cloud Management Platform across multiple CSPs (Azure, AWS, GCP, etc.)

• Provide analysis and recommendations for continuously improving cloud network and security (Azure, AWS, GCP, etc.)

• Maintain, deploy, upgrade, and troubleshoot the cloud platform network infrastructure of cloud-native and non-cloud-native solutions

• Collaborate with cyber and infrastructure teams, cloud service providers (CSPs), and vendors to develop cutting-edge technical solutions, implementation activities, and support processes

• Participate in the design, lifecycle management, and total cost of ownership of the cloud platform, applications, and infrastructure services

• Assist with problem escalation and operation tickets.

• Influence standards and platforms for future application development

• Write and manage technical documentation, policies, and procedures

• Design, develop, and implement automation scripts and workflows to deploy and manage cloud-based infrastructure and applications

• Collaborate with cross-functional teams to understand and translate system requirements into efficient and scalable automation solutions

• Stay current with new and evolving technologies via formal training and self-directed education

About the team

Deloitte Technology works at the forefront of technology development and processes to support and protect Deloitte around the world. In this truly global environment, we operate not in what is but rather what can be to help Deloitte deliver and connect with its clients, its communities, and one another in ways not previously conceived.

Enough about us, let’s talk about you

Required Qualifications and Experience :

• Minimum of 3 years’ experience designing and implementing public cloud platforms across at least two major Cloud Service Providers (Azure, AWS, or GCP).

• At least 4 years of hands-on experience in public cloud networking and security within Azure, AWS, or GCP environments.

• Strong expertise in networking and security engineering, including design, build, testing, and troubleshooting of complex large-scale enterprise and public cloud networks. Proficient with routing and switching, routing protocols (BGP, OSPF, IPv6), DNS, firewalls (e.g., Palo Alto, Check Point, cloud-native firewalls), Private Links, CDNs, Application Gateways, F5 load balancers, unified threat management, intrusion prevention systems (IPS), IPsec VPN tunnels, and Cisco routers and switches.

• Proven experience deploying hybrid cloud integrations
BYNZbho8bZVh1EPUAAAAAA==.reason: Strong network engineering background with BGP/MPLS aligns well with cloud network role, though lacks specific multi-cloud platform experience for senior level.

vI0FXRYZ1H5HfJ7jAAAAAA==.title: Network Engineer – SD-WAN​/Meraki​/Fortinet
vI0FXRYZ1H5HfJ7jAAAAAA==.company: Astra-North Infoteck Inc. ~ Conquering today’s challenges, achieving tomorrow’s vision!
vI0FXRYZ1H5HfJ7jAAAAAA==.description:
Position: Network Engineer – SD-WAN / Meraki / Fortinet
Role Overview
The Senior Network Engineer will be responsible for designing, implementing, maintaining, and supporting enterprise network infrastructure with a strong focus on SD-WAN, Cisco Meraki, Forti Gate firewalls, and cloud connectivity solutions. The role requires hands-on expertise in enterprise routing, switching, network security, monitoring, and large-scale network modernization initiatives.

Key Responsibilities
Enterprise Network Operations & Support

Provide day-to-day operational support, monitoring, maintenance, troubleshooting, and optimization of enterprise network environments.

Maintain network connectivity, cloud networking, and associated infrastructure solutions.

Create, maintain, and update enterprise network diagrams and technical documentation.

Design and implement scalable, fault-tolerant, and highly available network solutions.

SD-WAN & Network Engineering

Manage and optimize Cisco Meraki MX, MS, and MR networks using Meraki Dashboard.

Configure and support SD-WAN technologies including Cisco Viptela and Forti Gate SD-WAN.

Implement policy-based routing, static/dynamic routing, and route redistribution.

Design IP addressing strategies, redundancy, failover, and disaster recovery solutions.

Routing, Switching & Security

Strong expertise in TCP/IP, BGP, OSPF, MPLS, QoS, SDN, and SD-WAN.

Configure and support Cisco networking hardware including Catalyst, Nexus, and enterprise routers/switches.

Implement firewall, VPN, IDS/IPS, and encryption solutions.

Collaborate with Information Security teams to ensure compliance and secure network architecture.

Monitoring & Automation

Implement and maintain monitoring and alerting platforms using Solar Winds, Wireshark, and related tools.

Design dashboards, KPI reporting, and operational visibility metrics.

Participate in platform modernization, upgrades, and process improvements.

Leadership & Collaboration

Lead enterprise network initiatives and cross-functional implementation efforts.

Provide technical mentorship and architectural guidance to engineering teams.

Collaborate with business stakeholders to gather requirements and drive solution delivery.

Support medium and long-term enterprise network strategy planning.

Required Skills
Networking & SD-WAN

Cisco Meraki

Cisco Viptela

Forti Gate Firewalls

SD-WAN Technologies

Routing & Switching

TCP/IP

BGP

OSPF

MPLS

QoS

Security

VPN

IDS/IPS

Firewall Security

Encryption

Monitoring & Tools

Wireshark

Solar Winds

Network Monitoring & Alerting

Certifications Preferred

CCNA

CCNP

CCIE

ENSDWI (300-415)

NSE

Aruba Certifications

#J-18808-Ljbffr
vI0FXRYZ1H5HfJ7jAAAAAA==.reason: Strong technical match on BGP/MPLS/routing but lacks specific SD-WAN/Meraki/Fortinet experience and may be positioned as more senior than candidate's 3 years.

v_yrtHFb4XQBHOTKAAAAAA==.title: Engineer, Network (Junior)
v_yrtHFb4XQBHOTKAAAAAA==.description:
Rumble is a high-growth video platform and cloud services provider that is creating an independent infrastructure. Our mission is to restore the internet to its roots by making it free and open once again. About the Role

Rumble Cloud is seeking a Kubernetes Engineer to support our team in rolling out and operating our next-generation Kubernetes platform. This role will focus on our new CAPI/CAPO-based Kubernetes solution, which is designed to be compatible with our existing OpenStack Magnum API and will be deployed across our public cloud. You will help run the day-to-day operations of the Kubernetes service, assist with migrations and onboarding from our current Magnum-based offering, and act as an escalation point for complex customer issues that go beyond front-line support.

This is a hands‑on engineering role for someone who enjoys debugging difficult problems, improving reliability, and working closely with both platform engineers and customer‑facing teams.

Key Responsibilities Operate and maintain Rumble Cloud’s Kubernetes platform, including our new CAPI/CAPO-based installation and its integration with the OpenStack Magnum API. Assist in the rollout, migration, and upgrade processes as customers transition from the existing Magnum-based solution to the new platform. Serve as an escalation point for customer support, troubleshooting complex Kubernetes and cluster lifecycle issues.

Monitor the health, performance, and capacity of Kubernetes clusters and underlying infrastructure; assist in incident response and root cause analysis. Implement and maintain day‑2 operations processes, including backup/restore, scaling, patching, and cluster upgrades. Collaborate with the Kubernetes Architect to implement standards, best practices, and reference patterns for cluster configuration and operations.

Help manage and integrate container registries, identity, networking, and storage components required for the Kubernetes platform. Automate repetitive operational tasks using scripting and infrastructure‑as‑code tools. Contribute to and maintain runbooks, documentation, and knowledge base articles for both internal teams and customer‑facing support.

Provide feedback from operations and customer interactions to influence platform improvements and product roadmap.

Required Skills &

Experience Extensive experience in Linux systems engineering, cloud engineering, or platform/SRE roles, including hands‑on work with Kubernetes in production environments. Practical experience with installing, operating, and troubleshooting Kubernetes in production environments.

Experience working with Kubernetes on OpenStack or other cloud providers. Familiarity with Cluster API (CAPI) and/or Cluster API Provider OpenStack (CAPO), or strong willingness to ramp up quickly. Strong understanding of Linux, containers, and container runtimes (e.g., Docker, containerd).

Experience with Kubernetes networking, storage, and ingress concepts (CNI, CSI, load balancers, ingress controllers). Hands‑on experience with scripting and automation (e.g., Bash, Python, or Go) for operational tasks. Familiarity with configuration management and IaC tools such as Ansible and Terraform.

Experience with monitoring and logging in Kubernetes environments (e.g., Prometheus, Grafana, ELK/Graylog, or similar). Strong troubleshooting skills, clear written documentation, and effective communication with both technical and non‑technical stakeholders. Nice to Have (Preferred Skills) Experience with OpenStack Magnum and its Kubernetes integrations.

Experience operating Kubernetes on other public cloud platforms (EKS, AKS, GKE). Knowledge of Ceph, S3‑compatible storage, and their use with Kubernetes. Understanding of application architectures on Kubernetes, including microservices and 12‑factor applications. Exposure to CI/CD toolchains (e.g., Git, GitLab/Jenkins, Artifactory, etc.) for containerized workloads.

Experience with security hardening and best practices for Kubernetes
v_yrtHFb4XQBHOTKAAAAAA==.reason: Job requires Kubernetes/SRE expertise but candidate's background is network infrastructure (BGP/MPLS), not container orchestration or cloud platform engineering.

1pAYhSBTTnM2prwtAAAAAA==.title: Technical Sales Engineer & Fabrics & Rolls (Paper Machine Clothing)
1pAYhSBTTnM2prwtAAAAAA==.description:
Location

Location: Ontario, Canada (Greater Toronto Area preferred)

Territory: Ontario & Eastern Canada

Travel: Regional travel to customer mills required

About ANDRITZ

ANDRITZ Fabrics & Rolls is a global leader in paper machine clothing, roll technology, and performance optimization solutions for the pulp & paper industry. From the wet end to the dry end, our technologies help mills improve productivity, reliability, and operating efficiency.

What You’ll Do

We are seeking a Technical Sales Engineer to support our Fabrics & Rolls business in Ontario, partnering closely with paper mills to identify optimization opportunities, deliver value‑based solutions, and grow long‑term customer relationships. As a Technical Sales Engineer, you will serve as a trusted technical advisor to paper mills, combining hands‑on paper machine expertise with consultative sales to improve customer performance and drive ANDRITZ growth.

Customer Engagement & Technical Sales

• Build and maintain strong relationships with paper mill customers across Ontario and Eastern Canada

• Identify opportunities for paper machine clothing, roll covers, bowed rolls, and related services

• Conduct paper machine audits, service calls, and optimization studies

• Support customers during shutdown inspections, troubleshooting, and performance improvement initiatives

Optimization & Performance Support

• Perform paper machine optimization and press section studies, including Rezolve™ press optimization

• Analyze machine performance using proprietary modeling and diagnostic tools

• Troubleshoot operating challenges and recommend practical, value‑driven solutions

• Proactively identify issues to reduce warranty exposure and improve long‑term machine performance

Reporting & Collaboration

• Prepare clear, professional on‑site reports, recommendations, and follow‑ups

• Maintain detailed customer and machine records, including PMC, rolls, installations, and product life data

• Communicate field activity, opportunities, and competitive insights to the Sales Manager

• Provide monthly summaries and forecasts highlighting opportunities, mill conditions, and territory priorities

What We’re Looking For

• Degree in Engineering, Business, or equivalent experience

• Hands‑on experience in paper mills (production, maintenance, reliability, process, or technical sales)

• Strong knowledge of paper machine clothing, press sections, rolls, or machine optimization

• Proven ability to build relationships and communicate effectively at all mill levels

• Entrepreneurial, self‑driven, and comfortable working independently in the field

• Strong computer skills (Word, Excel, databases / CRM)

• Ability to travel within the assigned Ontario / Eastern Canada territory

Preferred

• Experience with paper machine clothing, rolls, or press optimization

• Background in technical sales, applications engineering, or field service

• Familiarity with paper machine audits and shutdown support

Why Join ANDRITZ Fabrics & Rolls

• Work with a globally recognized leader in paper machine performance

• Combine technical expertise with customer impact

• Long‑term customer relationships and stable territory

• Competitive compensation and expense support

• Strong technical, sales, and service team collaboration

Benefits

• Medical Dental

• Vision

• Life Insurance

• Disability Insurance

• 401k Match
